questions.question: The function f(x) = 3sin(2x − π/3) − 1 has a maximum value. At what y-value does this maximum occur?
questions.explanation: The maximum of sin(θ) is 1. So max of 3sin(2x − π/3) is 3(1) = 3. Then 3 − 1 = 2.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side a = 7, side b = 10, and angle C = 120°. Find side c using the law of cosines. Round to the nearest tenth.
The graph of y = −2cos(x) + 4 oscillates between which two y-values?
questions.explanation: cos(x) ranges from −1 to 1. So −2cos(x) ranges from 2 to −2. Adding 4 shifts the range to [2, 6].

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = 35°, angle Q = 65°, and side p = 12. Using the law of sines, find side q. Round to the nearest integer.
The function y = tan(x) has a vertical asymptote at x = π/2. At what x-value in the interval (π/2, 3π/2) does the next vertical asymptote occur?
questions.explanation: Vertical asymptotes of tan(x) occur at x = π/2 + nπ for integer n. For n = 1: x = π/2 + π = 3π/2.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, sides a = 8, b = 6, and c = 7. Use the law of cosines to find cos(A), then express your answer as a simplified fraction.
